quoteLast: , and are despatched to London and Shrewsbury at 4.50 P.M., Birmingham 8.45 A.M., Hereford, 2.47 P.M., and Worcester at 1.30 P.M.—_Eleanor Sankey_, _post-mistress_.
title: History, Gazetteer, and Directory of Shropshire [1851]
author: Bagshaw, Samuel

quoteFirst: Captain D. F. Stiles, of the Tenth Infantry, who had been made provost marshal of the new Territory, and whose soldiers guarded the land before and maintained peace after the invasion, raised his telescope at
quoteLast: on the eventful 22d of April, and saw nothing from the station to the horizon but an empty green prairie of high waving grass.
title: The West from a car window
author: Davis, Richard Harding, 1864-1916; Remington, Frederic, 1861-1909 [Illustrator]

quoteFirst: "I was called by Constable Simmonds at eleven-forty-nine, and reached the house at
quoteLast: in company with Inspector Harris and Divisional Surgeon Davidson. When I arrived Dr. Hart, Dr. Thorndyke, and Dr. Jervis were already in the room. I found the deceased woman, Minna Adler, lying in bed with her throat cut. She was dead and cold. There were no signs of a struggle, and the bed did not appear to have been disturbed. There was a table by the bedside on which was a book and an empty candlestick. The candle had apparently burnt out, for there was only a piece of charred wick at the bottom of the socket. A box had been placed on the floor at the head of the bed and a hassock stood on it.
title:
John Thorndyke's Cases
related by Christopher Jervis and edited by R. Austin Freeman
author: Freeman, R. Austin (Richard Austin), 1862-1943; Brock, H. M. (Henry Matthew), 1875-1960 [Illustrator]

quoteFirst: In the morning I went on to the Allan Liner _Sardinian_, when we left Quebec
quoteLast: I had an intermediate ticket: it was quite as good as first-class.
title:
The Strand Magazine, Vol. 07, Issue 39, March 1894
An Illustrated Monthly
author: Various; Newnes, George, 1851-1910 [Editor]

quoteFirst: The Duchess had taken no notice of this letter, and I concluded sadly that I was never to see her or hear of her again; but as I was sitting by myself in Jasmine's inner drawing-room on that same 1st of August,
quoteTime: about twelve o'clock in the morning
quoteLast: , I was startled when the door was thrown open, and the dear Duchess came in.
title: A Plucky Girl
author: Meade, L. T., 1854-1914

quoteLast: the door-bell rang, and a look overspread the lady's face that was neither maternal, sisterly, nor amorous; but partook in an indescribable manner of all three kinds.
title: Two on a Tower
author: Hardy, Thomas, 1840-1928
